metadata.title: Atezolizumab, CARBOplatin AUC 5 and Etoposide 100 mg/m² — 21 day
metadata.indication: First-line extensive-stage small-cell lung cancer, with four induction cycles followed by atezolizumab maintenance.
metadata.safety_note: Cytotoxic ANC/platelet rules are restricted to induction. Maintenance uses atezolizumab immune-toxicity logic only.

clinical_governance.prescriptive_authority: Treatment plan must be initiated by a Consultant Medical Oncologist.
clinical_governance.dose_modification_note: Any dose modification should be discussed with a Consultant.
clinical_governance.disclaimer: Decision-support encoding of the NCCP source document. It does not replace the current NCCP regimen, clinical judgement, local policy, prescribing review, or pharmacy verification.

indications.description: First-line treatment of adult patients with extensive-stage small-cell lung cancer.

options.label: 00689a — First-line treatment of adult patients with extensive-stage small-cell lung cancer.
IV day 1 and oral twice daily on days 2 and 3

active_infection_requiring_therapy.label: Active clinically significant infection requiring therapy

pneumonitis_grade.label: Immune-mediated pneumonitis / interstitial lung disease grade

creatinine_ratio_baseline_or_uln.label: Serum creatinine relative to baseline or ULN

tsh_miu_l.label: TSH
tsh_miu_l.help: Interpret with free T4 and symptoms; no universal numeric hold threshold is encoded.

free_t4_pmol_l.label: Free T4
free_t4_pmol_l.help: Interpret with TSH and symptoms; no universal numeric hold threshold is encoded.

cortisol_nmol_l.label: Cortisol
cortisol_nmol_l.help: Interpret according to sampling time, symptoms and local endocrine guidance.

endocrine_status.label: Immune-mediated endocrine status
endocrine_status.help: Use the clinical syndrome and laboratory pattern together.

rash_grade_2_over_one_week.label: Grade 2 rash lasting more than one week

serious_neurological_irae_grade.label: Serious immune-mediated neurological syndrome grade
serious_neurological_irae_grade.help: For syndromes such as myasthenia gravis, encephalitis, Guillain–Barré syndrome, myelitis or meningitis.

fbc_clinical_concern.label: FBC result requiring clinical review (no protocol numerical ICI hold threshold)

rule_engine.missing_data_policy: Missing optional inputs are never interpreted as normal; report unassessed domains.

action.recommendation: Do not administer atezolizumab; obtain consultant review.

rules.explanation: Relevant hypersensitivity is an exclusion.

action.recommendation: Do not proceed without urgent consultant review of pregnancy or breastfeeding risk.

rules.explanation: Pregnancy or breastfeeding requires specialist review.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab and treat/review the infection until clinically stable.

rules.explanation: Clinically significant active infection requires review before treatment.

action.recommendation: Review the FBC abnormality clinically; no protocol-defined ANC or platelet hold threshold is encoded for single-agent atezolizumab.

rules.explanation: FBC is monitored, but NCCP 00593 does not specify an ANC or platelet treatment threshold.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab and manage as immune-mediated pneumonitis.

rules.explanation: Grade 2 pneumonitis requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for grade 3–4 pneumonitis.

rules.explanation: Grade 3–4 pneumonitis requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab and manage as immune-mediated hepatitis.

rules.explanation: ALT/AST >3–5×ULN or bilirubin >1.5–3×ULN requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for severe immune-mediated hepatitis.

rules.explanation: ALT/AST >5×ULN or bilirubin >3×ULN requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab and manage the immune-mediated diarrhoea/colitis pathway.

rules.explanation: Grade 2–3 immune-mediated diarrhoea/colitis requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for grade 4 diarrhoea/colitis.

rules.explanation: Grade 4 diarrhoea/colitis requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab until symptomatic thyroid dysfunction is controlled and thyroid function is improving.

rules.explanation: Symptomatic hypothyroidism or hyperthyroidism requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab until clinically stable on appropriate replacement therapy.

rules.explanation: Symptomatic adrenal insufficiency or grade 2–3 hypophysitis requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Controlled endocrinopathy on replacement therapy does not automatically require permanent discontinuation; confirm stability.

rules.explanation: Controlled endocrinopathies are an exception to the general permanent-discontinuation rule.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab and urgently establish metabolic control with insulin.

rules.explanation: Suspected immune-mediated diabetes with glucose >13.9 mmol/L meets the grade 3–4 hyperglycaemia pathway.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab and refer for specialist assessment of suspected SCAR.

rules.explanation: Suspected SJS/TEN or another SCAR requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for confirmed SJS/TEN or another severe cutaneous adverse reaction.

rules.explanation: Confirmed SJS/TEN requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab for grade 3 immune-mediated rash.

rules.explanation: Grade 3 rash requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for grade 4 rash.

rules.explanation: Grade 4 rash requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab and manage the immune-mediated pancreatitis pathway.

rules.explanation: Grade 2–3 pancreatitis or amylase/lipase >2×ULN with grade 3–4 elevation requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for grade 4 or recurrent pancreatitis.

rules.explanation: Grade 4 pancreatitis requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab and urgently manage suspected immune-mediated myocarditis.

rules.explanation: Grade 2 or above myocarditis requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab and manage as immune-mediated nephritis.

rules.explanation: Creatinine >1.5–3× baseline or ULN meets the grade 2 nephritis pathway.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for grade 3–4 nephritis.

rules.explanation: Creatinine >3× baseline or ULN requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab and manage immune-mediated myositis.

rules.explanation: Grade 2–3 myositis requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for grade 4 or recurrent grade 3 myositis.

rules.explanation: Grade 4 or recurrent grade 3 myositis requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab and urgently manage the serious neurological immune-mediated syndrome.

rules.explanation: Serious neurological immune-mediated syndromes generally require permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab and obtain detailed cardiac assessment.

rules.explanation: Grade 1 pericardial disorder requires withholding and cardiac evaluation.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for grade 2 or higher pericardial disorder.

rules.explanation: Grade 2 or higher pericardial disorder requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab and urgently assess/manage suspected HLH.

rules.explanation: Suspected haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Withhold atezolizumab until recovery to grade 0–1 and steroid reduction as specified.

rules.explanation: Grade 2–3 other immune-mediated toxicity requires withholding.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for grade 4 immune-mediated toxicity, except controlled endocrinopathies.

rules.explanation: Grade 4 other immune-mediated toxicity requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for recurrent grade 3 immune-mediated toxicity.

rules.explanation: Recurrent grade 3 immune-mediated toxicity requires permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Reduce the infusion rate or interrupt; treatment may resume once the event resolves.

rules.explanation: Grade 1–2 infusion reactions require slowing or interruption.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab for a grade 3–4 infusion reaction.

rules.explanation: Grade 3–4 infusion reactions require permanent discontinuation.

action.recommendation: Delay induction chemotherapy one week until recovery.

rules.explanation: Induction ANC 0.5 to <1.0 requires delay.

action.recommendation: Delay induction chemotherapy one week until recovery.

rules.explanation: Induction platelets 75 to <100 require delay.

action.recommendation: Delay induction chemotherapy and consider reducing carboplatin and etoposide by 25% for subsequent cycles.

rules.explanation: Severe cytopenia or febrile neutropenia requires delay and reduction review.

action.recommendation: Do not administer carboplatin at GFR ≤20 mL/min.

rules.explanation: Carboplatin is not administered at GFR ≤20.

action.recommendation: Use carboplatin with extreme caution at GFR 20–30 mL/min.

rules.explanation: Severe renal impairment requires specialist review.

action.recommendation: Use etoposide at 75% of the original dose and increase if tolerated.

rules.explanation: NCCP renal dose modification for etoposide.

action.recommendation: Consider etoposide at 50% of the original dose and increase if tolerated.

rules.explanation: Bilirubin ≥50 µmol/L or decreased albumin requires etoposide reduction review.

action.recommendation: Etoposide is not dialysed; consider 75% of the original dose after oncology-pharmacy review.

rules.explanation: Haemodialysis requires etoposide dose review.

action.recommendation: No restrictive encoded rule was triggered by the entered assessment data.

rules.explanation: Fallback proceed finding; any restrictive matched rule takes precedence.

output_templates.proceed: No encoded hold, discontinuation, or modification criterion was triggered by the entered data.
output_templates.coverage_gap: One or more optional clinical domains were not assessed. Missing values have not been interpreted as normal.
